title: Long Diagonal Sweep
setup: A white bishop on **f3** stares down the long **a8-h1 diagonal** at an undefended black rook.
hint: Look for undefended pieces on your bishop's diagonals — long-range collectors.
explanation:
## The Idea

This is a simple but vital pattern: an **undefended (loose) piece** on a long diagonal. The bishop on f3 sees all the way to h1, where the black rook is stranded with no friendly defender nearby.

White plays {{Bxh1}} and simply walks off with the rook.

- The black king is on d5, far too distant to recapture.
- No black piece defends h1.
- The rook cannot move out of the way **before** white's move — White has the turn.

## Pattern to Memorize

> Loose Pieces Drop Off — the **LPDO** rule.

Before every move, scan your bishop's and rook's open lines for enemy pieces with no defender. They are free material waiting to be collected.
